segments.p1-3-g37: Ну‑с is a colloquial, slightly old-fashioned interjection (ну + particle -с) equivalent to “well now” or “well then”; it simply signals a transition or softens what follows rather than contributing propositional meaning.
segments.p1-3-g38: Такого‑то вот непосредственного человека is a strongly marked direct object (animate masculine: человека in the accusative, which looks like genitive) composed with emphatic particles (-то on такого and вот). Непосредственного qualifies человека. After the verb считать the predicate (настоящим) appears in the instrumental case; the particle и here is emphatic (“indeed / even”) rather than coordinating.
segments.p1-3-g39: Настоящим, нормальным человеком are predicate complements in the instrumental case (считать кого кем), giving two coordinated qualities: 'a real, normal man'. The comma separates the two evaluative terms for emphasis.
segments.p1-3-g40: Каким introduces a relative (or exclamative) clause agreeing implicitly in case with 'человеком' (i.e., 'the sort of man'). Хотела is past tense, feminine singular, agreeing with the subject that follows (сама нежная мать — природа); его is the object of видеть. Word order is inverted for stylistic emphasis: the verb and its complements come before the explicit subject.
segments.p1-3-g41: Любезно зарождая ... is a de‑eppричастный (gerund/adverbial participle) clause describing manner — 'kindly/affectionately bringing him into the world.' The present gerund (зарождая) indicates an attendant or characteristic action connected to the main clause (how Nature 'brought him to be'); зарождать is imperfective, giving a general or habitual tone.
segments.p1-3-g42: Я is the subject; такому человеку is in the dative because завидовать governs the dative ('to envy someone'). До крайней желчи is a figurative measure phrase (до + genitive) meaning 'to extreme bitterness'; завидую is present first‑person singular. The whole is a concise clause: 'I envy such a person to extreme bitterness.'
segments.p1-3-g43: Он is the nominative personal pronoun 'he' and is the sentence subject. Глуп is the short-form predicate adjective (masculine singular), used instead of full глупый to form the simple predication 'he is stupid.'
segments.p1-3-g44: Я is the first-person subject. В этом is a prepositional phrase ('about that / in this') where этом refers back to the previous statement; с вами ('with you') is an additional adverbial. Не спорю is the 1st-person singular present of спорить with negation 'I do not argue'. Word order places the adverbials before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p1-3-g45: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but', marking the contrast with the preceding concession.
segments.p1-3-g46: Может быть is a fixed modal phrase formed from the verb мочь + infinitive быть and functions parenthetically as 'maybe' or 'perhaps' (third-person impersonal possibility). The comma signals its parenthetical use.
segments.p1-3-g47: Нормальный человек is the nominative NP 'a normal person'. И here is emphatic/colloquial ('and/even'), linking to должен быть 'ought to be': должен is a modal adjective meaning 'ought to' and is followed by the infinitive быть; the clause ends with the short-form adjective глуп as predicative ('ought to be stupid').
segments.p1-3-g48: Почему is the interrogative 'why' placed at the front for a rhetorical question. Вы is the addressed 'you' (formal/plural). Знаете is the 2nd-person plural present of знать; here it functions as a colloquial tag ('you know? / are you aware?') rather than a literal factual question.
segments.p1-3-g49: Может быть is the same parenthetical modal phrase 'maybe / it may be,' indicating possibility. It stands apart with the comma.
segments.p1-3-g50: Это is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' serving as the subject. Даже 'even' and очень 'very' are adverbial intensifiers modifying красиво, which is a predicative form meaning 'beautiful' (here used as a predicate/adverbial quality).
segments.p1-3-g83: Взглянем is a first-person-plural hortative/imperative ('let us look'), used here to invite the reader to observe. же is an emphatic particle strengthening the exhortation; теперь places the action in the present moment. на эту мышь is a preposition + accusative phrase ('at this mouse / at this timid person') and в действии is a prepositional phrase ('in action') describing how we should look at her.
segments.p1-3-g84: Положим here is a hortative/introductory verb meaning 'let us suppose' or 'suppose that' and introduces a hypothetical example; it functions like an English 'suppose' or 'let's say'.
segments.p1-3-g85: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' that signals the following clause is an illustration of the point just made.
segments.p1-3-g86: Она тоже обижена: она is the subject 'she', тоже 'also/too', and обижена is a short passive/adjectival form meaning 'is offended/is wronged' (predicate). The parenthetical (а она почти всегда бывает обижена) repeats and emphasizes that this is habitual: бывает here means 'is/comes to be (habitually)'. И тоже желает отомстить: and likewise (и тоже) желает is present-tense 'wants/desires' followed by the perfective infinitive отомстить 'to avenge / take revenge'.
segments.p1-3-g87: Злости-то в ней is an inverted, colloquial-styled phrase: злости is the genitive of 'anger/malice' meaning 'of anger' and в ней 'in her'; the enclitic -то adds a slight emphasis or contrast ('as for the anger in her').
segments.p1-3-g88: Может is a parenthetical modal 'may' or 'might' indicating possibility; it softens the assertion that follows.
segments.p1-3-g89: Ещё и больше накопится is a predicate: ещё и больше ('even more, still more') intensifies the comparative, and накопится is a perfective reflexive future meaning 'will accumulate' or 'will be accumulated', so the clause says 'may accumulate even more'.
segments.p1-3-g90: Чем в ... introduces the standard comparative 'than in ...' with чем as 'than' and в as the preposition 'in' governing the following (French) phrase.
segments.p1-3-g91: Гадкое is an attributive epithet 'nasty' (neuter form) standing alone with a comma to qualify the following noun phrase; it's an emphatic adjective remarking on the quality of the desire.
segments.p1-3-g92: Низкое желаньице is a diminutive noun phrase 'a low/base little desire'; воздать is the infinitive 'to render/to give back' and takes a dative object обидчику 'to the offender'; тем же злом uses the demonstrative тем + emphatic же and the instrumental злом 'with the same evil' to express 'to repay with the same harm'.
segments.p1-3-g93: Может here again is the modal 'may/might', signaling that what follows is possible or conjectural.
segments.p1-3-g94: Ещё и гаже скребется в ней: ещё и гаже ('even worse, still more hideously') intensifies гаже 'worse'; скребется is a reflexive imperfective verb meaning 'scrapes/gnaws about/torments itself' (here idiomatic for inner unrest), with в ней 'in her' locating the action internally.
segments.p1-3-g95: Чем в ... is the comparative connector 'than in ...' repeating the structure from the previous sentence and pointing to the contrasted subject.
segments.p1-3-g96: Потому что is the causal conjunction 'because' (потому 'for that reason' + что 'that'), introducing the explanation that follows for why the comparison holds.
segments.p1-3-g97: По своей врожденной глупости is an ablative/causal phrase 'because of/by virtue of his innate stupidity': по + dative/possessive phrase here expresses cause or attribution; врожденной is the adjective 'innate/congenital' modifying глупости 'stupidity'.
segments.p1-3-g98: Считает свое мщенье просто-запросто справедливостью is a clause where считает 'considers' takes a direct object phrase: свое мщенье 'his own vengeance' and a predicative noun справедливостью 'as justice'; просто-запросто is a colloquial intensive pairing 'simply/quite simply' reinforcing that he takes vengeance to be plain justice.
segments.p1-3-g99: А мышь is the contrasting topic 'but the mouse' (а = 'but/whereas'); мышь here is used metaphorically for 'the timid person/creature' rather than the literal rodent.
segments.p1-3-g100: Вследствие усиленного сознания is an instrumental/causal phrase 'as a result of / owing to intensified consciousness': вследствие 'due to', усиленного is the genitive of the participle 'intensified', and сознания is genitive 'consciousness/awareness'.
segments.p1-3-g101: Отрицает тут справедливость is a simple clause: отрицает 'denies/negates' with direct object справедливость 'justice'; тут 'here / at this point' signals that the denial applies in the present context or argument.
segments.p1-3-g19: "Для них" is a prepositional phrase meaning "for them" and sits before the subject; the dash after "стена" links the subject to a nominal predicate "не отвод" (a noun phrase with negation) — literally "the wall is not a diversion/excuse."
segments.p1-3-g20: "как например для нас" is a short comparative clause: "as, for example, for us." "как" introduces the comparison, "например" is a parenthetical "for example," and "для нас" is the prepositional object "for us."
segments.p1-3-g21: "людей думающих" is a genitive plural noun "people" modified by a present active participle "думающих" (thinking); the participle follows the noun and describes the kind of people ("people who think").
segments.p1-3-g22: "а следственно" links and contrasts the clauses: "а" = "and/but," while "следственно" is an adverb meaning "consequently/therefore," supplying the inference that follows (they think and, consequently...).
segments.p1-3-g23: "ничего не делающих" is a participial phrase (object of the earlier "людей") formed with the negative particle "ничего" + "не" + present active participle "делающих," literally "doing nothing" — i.e. "who do nothing."
segments.p1-3-g24: "не предлог воротиться с дороги" is an appositive phrase: "не предлог" = "not a pretext," followed by the infinitive "воротиться" (to turn/return) which expresses the action tied to that pretext; "с дороги" = "from the road" ("с" = from + genitive).
segments.p1-3-g25: A repeated noun "предлог" in apposition ("a pretext") — the comma shows it restates/emphasizes the previous idea and prepares the relative clause that follows.
segments.p1-3-g26: "в который ... не верит" is a relative clause introduced by dative/locative "в который" (here functioning as "in/which/that"); "наш брат" is the subject, and "обыкновенно и сам не верит" means "ordinarily does not himself believe it," where "и сам" adds emphatic "even he himself."
segments.p1-3-g27: "но которому всегда очень рад" continues the relative construction: "но" = "but," "которому" (dative) refers back to "предлог" ("to which"), and the short-form adjective "рад" (predicative) with modifiers "всегда очень" means "is always very glad (of it)."
segments.p1-3-g28: "Нет" is an emphatic interjection here correcting or opposing the previous idea: "No" or "No —" used to introduce the speaker's contrary point.
segments.p1-3-g29: "они пасуют" is third-person plural present; here "пасуют" means "they yield/back down" (an idiomatic meaning). The phrase "со всею искренностью" uses the variant preposition "со" + instrumental "искренностью" with the archaic/dialectal feminine instrumental "всею" meaning "with all/complete sincerity."
segments.p1-3-g30: "Стена имеет для них" is a simple clause "the wall has for them" ("иметь" + indirect object "для них"); "что-то успокоительное" is an indefinite phrase "something calming/soothing," where "что-то" is an indefinite pronoun and "успокоительное" is a neuter adjective/noun meaning "soothing thing."
segments.p1-3-g31: "нравственно-разрешающее и окончательное" are participial/adjectival modifiers in a row describing that "something": "нравственно-разрешающее" = "morally permitting/authorizing" (gives moral license) and "окончательное" = "final/definitive."
segments.p1-3-g32: "пожалуй" is a parenthetical adverb meaning "perhaps/it may be," signaling a hedge or slight uncertainty about the following addition.
segments.p1-3-g33: "даже что-то мистическое" adds an emphatic unexpected quality: "даже" = "even," "что-то" = "something," and "мистическое" = "mystical"; together: "perhaps even something mystical."
segments.p1-3-g36: "Но" = "but" introduces a contrast or postponement; "об стене" is the prepositional phrase "about the wall," and "после" here is adverbial "later/afterwards," so the whole elliptical phrase promises to discuss the wall later (the verb is purposely omitted and the parenthetical ")" closes the aside).
segments.p1-3-g102: Доходит is 3rd person singular present of доходить used impersonally here: “(it) reaches/comes (to).” Наконец is an adverb meaning “finally.” До governs the genitive and here marks extent (“up to”), самoго is the emphatic genitive form “the very,” and дела is genitive singular of дело meaning “the matter/point.”
segments.p1-3-g103: Another prepositional phrase with до + genitive: до (up to), самого (emphatic “the very”), акта (genitive singular of акт, “deed/act”) and отмщения (genitive of отмщение, “revenge/ retribution”) — the phrase specifies the ultimate point reached.
segments.p1-3-g104: Несчастная мышь is the nominative subject (“the wretched/poor mouse”). Кроме + genitive (одной первоначальной гадости) means “besides/except for one original nastiness.” Успела (past, feminine) + infinitive нагородить forms the perfective-past meaning “managed to heap up/produce”; кругом себя is an adverbial/accusative cluster meaning “around herself.”
segments.p1-3-g105: В виде is a fixed expression meaning “in the form/guise of,” followed by the genitive plural вопросов и сомнений (“questions and doubts”) that name the form in which those nastinesses appear.
segments.p1-3-g106: Столько here means “so many/so much”; других (genitive plural) qualifies гадостей (genitive plural of гадость), i.e. “so many other nasty things.” The phrase is a loose apposition to what she has ‘nagorodit’ around herself.
segments.p1-3-g107: К + dative (к одному вопросу) means “toward/with respect to one question.” Подвела is past feminine of подвести meaning “led/ brought (something) to” (here: led one question on to …). Столько неразрешённых вопросов is the object in genitive plural meaning “so many unresolved questions.”
segments.p1-3-g108: Что introduces the result clause (“so that / so much that”). Поневоле means “inevitably/against one’s will.” Кругом нее literally “around her” (кругом + pronoun in genitive), and набирается (3rd sg) is a reflexive/impersonal verb meaning “gathers/accumulates.” Какая-то is the indeterminate pronoun “some / a kind of,” followed by роковая бурда, a collocation meaning “a fateful/ disastrous mess/drivel.”
segments.p1-3-g109: Repetition of какая-то = “some sort of” + adjective вонючая “stinking/disgusting” modifying грязь (noun, “filth/dirt”); together: “some kind of stinking filth.”
segments.p1-3-g110: Состоящая is a present participle (agreeing with грязь, feminine) from состоять meaning “consisting of.” Из introduces the material or components (“out of/of”), ее is the possessive pronoun “her,” and сомнений is genitive plural “doubts.”
segments.p1-3-g111: Волнений is genitive plural of волнение here meaning “anxieties/worries”; и simply links this element with the following ones in the list that compose the filth.
segments.p1-3-g112: Наконец is an adverb here introducing the final element in the list — “and, finally.”
segments.p1-3-g113: Из + genitive (плевков) marks another component: плевков can be literal “spits” or figurative “insults/contempt”; here it names the contemptuous things directed at her.
segments.p1-3-g114: Сыплющихся is a present participle (plural genitive to agree with плевков understood) from сыпаться meaning “to pour/come thick” (i.e. are falling/poured) and describes the plевки; на нее is the direction “at her,” от marks the source “from,” непосредственных qualifies деятелей (“the direct/immediate doers/actors”).
segments.p1-3-g115: This participial clause modifies деятелей: предстоящих (from предстоять) = “standing before/standing around,” торжественно = “solemnly/ triumphantly,” кругом = “around,” в виде = “in the guise/appearance of,” судей и диктаторов = “judges and dictators” (genitive plural because governed by в виде), and хохочущих (present participle) = “laughing derisively” with над нею (over her). Во всю здоровую глотку is an idiomatic adverbial phrase meaning “at the top of their lungs” (literally “with the whole big throat”), where здоровую here conveys ‘big/strong.’
segments.p1-3-g116: Разумеется is a discourse adverb meaning “of course” or “naturally,” used to introduce an expected result.
segments.p1-3-g117: Ей остается + infinitive is an impersonal construction meaning “what remains for her (to do) is …”; махнуть на всё (махнуть + на + accusative) means “to wave/dismiss at everything,” своей лапкой is an instrumental phrase “with her little paw,” and с улыбкой напускного презренья is another instrumental phrase indicating manner: “with a smile of affected/put-on contempt.”
segments.p1-3-g118: Которому is a relative pronoun in the dative agreeing with презренью (to/for which); верить requires the dative, so this clause means “which even she herself does not believe” (и сама она adds emphatic ‘even she herself’).
segments.p1-3-g119: Постыдно is an adverb “shamefully.” Проскользнуть is perfective infinitive “to slip/slide in,” and в свою щелочку uses в + accusative to indicate motion into her own diminutive щелочка (“little crack/crevice”), completing what remains for her to do.
segments.p1-3-g120: Там is the locative adverb 'there', setting the spatial scene for the whole clause.
segments.p1-3-g121: В (in) governs the following noun phrase. Своем is the possessive pronoun in the appropriate case agreeing with подполье ('its/one's own'). Мерзком is an adjective in the same case agreeing with the noun; together they form 'in its nasty ...'.
segments.p1-3-g122: Вонючем is an adjective agreeing in case, number, and gender with the following noun. Подполье is a neuter noun here meaning a literal cellar/underground place ('cellar/underground'), the head of the locative phrase 'in its nasty, stinking underground.'
segments.p1-3-g123: Наша is the possessive pronoun 'our' agreeing with the feminine participle обиженная; обиженная is a past passive participle used substantively/adjectivally ('the offended one'). The phrase is an appositive describing the mouse that follows.
segments.p1-3-g124: Прибитая и осмеянная are past passive participles ('beaten-down/pinned' and 'mocked') modifying мышь ('mouse'), which is the subject. Немедленно is the adverb 'immediately'; погружается is the 3rd-person singular present reflexive verb 'plunges / sinks into' (погружаться + reflexive), and в холодную begins the accusative phrase 'into cold ...' that takes the object злость later.
segments.p1-3-g125: Ядовитую is an accusative feminine adjective 'poisonous/spiteful' modifying the forthcoming noun; и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking the adjectives.
segments.p1-3-g126: Главное is a parenthetical nominal/adverbial meaning 'the main thing' or 'above all'; it's used here to signal that what follows is the chief characteristic.
segments.p1-3-g127: Вековечную is the accusative feminine adjective 'everlasting / age-old' agreeing with злость, the accusative feminine noun meaning 'anger / malice' which is the object of погружается в. Together they complete the phrase 'plunges into ... anger.'
segments.p1-3-g1: 'Ведь' is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' that cushions the question; 'у людей' is a prepositional phrase with у + genitive meaning 'among/with people' (introducing the class of people being talked about). The comma prepares for the following participial clause that qualifies 'людей'.
segments.p1-3-g2: 'умеющих' is a present active participle (genitive plural) modifying 'людей' and expresses capability ('who can/know how to ...'); the infinitive phrase 'за себя отомстить' ('to avenge oneself') is the action they are able to do, and 'и вообще за себя постоять' adds another infinitive ('and in general to stand up for oneself') with 'вообще' as an adverbial qualifier. The two infinitives are coordinated by 'и'.
segments.p1-3-g3: The dash marks a pause before the question. 'как' is the interrogative 'how' and 'это' is the dummy demonstrative 'it/this' referring to the situation just described ('how is that'). Together they introduce the main interrogative clause.
segments.p1-3-g4: 'например' is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' adding that the following question is illustrative.
segments.p1-3-g5: 'делается' is the 3rd-person singular present of the imperfective verb 'делаться' used impersonally/passively here ('is done' / 'happens'). In the interrogative it asks how the situation operates or comes about: 'how is that done?'.
segments.p1-3-g6: 'Ведь' again is an emphasizing particle ('after all'). 'их' is the accusative object 'them'. The colloquial sequence 'как обхватит' uses 'как' in the temporal sense 'as soon as/when' plus the perfective future/present verb 'обхватит' ('seize, grip') to mean 'as soon as the feeling seizes them'.
segments.p1-3-g7: 'положим' here is parenthetical: 'let us suppose/let's say' (it does not mean literally 'put'); it softens or frames the example just given.
segments.p1-3-g8: The noun phrase 'чувство мести' is the grammatical subject of 'обхватит' ('the feeling of revenge seizes them'). 'чувство' is 'feeling' and 'мести' is the genitive 'of revenge'.
segments.p1-3-g9: This clause states the consequence. 'так уж' is a combined particle ('then/indeed') emphasizing the result; 'ничего больше' ('nothing else') is the negative pronoun + modifier; Russian uses double negation here with 'ничего' and the verb negated by 'не' later. 'во всем их существе' = 'throughout their whole being' (locative expression), 'на это время' = 'for that time/at that time', and 'не останется' is future (or perfective result) 'will not remain'.
segments.p1-3-g10: 'кроме этого чувства' is a prepositional/genitive phrase meaning 'except for this feeling' (кроме + genitive). 'этого' modifies 'чувства' in the genitive to point back to the just-mentioned feeling.
segments.p1-3-g11: 'Такой господин' = 'such a gentleman/man'. The phrase 'так и' is an idiomatic intensifier ('just/simply'), and 'прет' is a colloquial present-tense verb meaning 'rushes/charges' (note: colloquial; not to be confused with other verbs spelled the same). 'прямо к цели' indicates direction 'straight toward the goal'.
segments.p1-3-g12: 'как' introduces a simile 'like'; 'взбесившийся' is a past active participle ('having gone mad/enraged') modifying 'бык' and describes the manner of charging: 'like an enraged bull'.
segments.p1-3-g13: 'наклонив' is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'having lowered/bent'; it modifies the main action to describe the bull's posture. 'вниз рога' ('its horns down') completes the image: 'lowering its horns downward'.
segments.p1-3-g14: 'и' links the clauses; 'только разве' softens the following statement to 'only perhaps' or 'and only, perhaps'; word order 'стена его останавливает' places the subject 'стена' first, the object pronoun 'его' after it (stressed), and the verb 'останавливает' at the end — literal: 'the wall stops him'.
segments.p1-3-g15: 'Кстати' is a parenthetical adverb 'by the way' introducing an incidental remark; the opening parenthesis shows this is an aside.
segments.p1-3-g16: 'перед стеной' is a prepositional phrase 'before/in front of a wall'; 'такие господа' is the noun phrase 'such gentlemen' in nominative plural — the topic of this aside.
segments.p1-3-g17: 'то есть' = 'that is' (restating/clarifying); 'непосредственные люди и деятели' is an appositive clarification: 'straightforward/direct people and doers/men of action', where 'непосредственные' qualifies 'люди' and 'деятели' repeats the idea in a more specific register.
segments.p1-3-g18: 'искренно' is an adverb 'sincerely/earnestly'; 'пасуют' is the 3rd-person plural present of the colloquial verb 'пасовать' meaning 'to falter/lose nerve/stand down' (not the card-game sense). The verb agrees with 'такие господа' (they).
segments.p1-3-g128: Сорок лет сряду is a fronted temporal phrase meaning 'for forty years in a row' (сряду = 'in a row'). будет припоминать is the compound future (будет + infinitive) expressing a continuing/repeated future action 'will keep recalling'; до последних is a prepositional/limit expression 'up to the last [ones]' and anticipates the noun (подробностей) in the next segment.
segments.p1-3-g129: самых постыдных подробностей is a genitive phrase ('the very most shameful details') modifying припоминать до; свою обиду is the direct object 'her own grievance' (accusative). и при этом ('and in doing so' / 'and at the same time') links to the following clause; каждый раз is 'each time' and прибавлять от себя is an infinitive phrase 'to add from herself' (i.e., to append on her own), while еще постыднейшие is an intensifying comparative/superlative 'even more shameful particulars'.
segments.p1-3-g130: злобно is an adverb 'maliciously' modifying the gerund поддразнивая; поддразнивая and раздражая are adverbial participles (деепричастия) expressing concurrent actions 'maliciously teasing and irritating' and both take себя as their object. собственной фантазией is instrumental 'with her own imagination' indicating the means by which she taunts and irritates herself.
segments.p1-3-g131: Сама is an emphatic pronoun 'she herself' (emphasizing the subject). будет стыдиться is the compound future ('will be ashamed of'); стыдиться governs the genitive, hence своей фантазии (genitive) 'of her imagination'.
segments.p1-3-g132: но все-таки is a concessive connective 'but nevertheless / still' (таки is colloquial/emphatic). все припомнит uses the perfective припомнить in future 'will recall everything' — a single completed recalling contrasted with the continuous recalling in the previous sentence.
segments.p1-3-g133: все переберет repeats 'everything' and uses переберет (perfective future) meaning 'will go through / sift through / run through mentally' — here mental review, not literal plucking.
segments.p1-3-g134: навыдумает is a perfective verb 'will invent / will imagine up' (with an intensive prefix на-). на себя means 'about/on herself' ('directed onto herself'). небывальщины is the object 'tall tales / impossible things' (accusative), the content she invents.
segments.p1-3-g135: под предлогом is a prepositional phrase using под + instrumental meaning 'under the pretext (of...)' and introduces the reason/excuse for the invention that follows.
segments.p1-3-g136: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause; она refers back to the invented thing (generic 'it') and тожe means 'also / too'; могла случиться is past tense of мочь + infinitive 'случиться' and here expresses possibility in the past or 'could/might have happened.'
segments.p1-3-g137: и connects the clause and ничего не простит is a standard negation 'will forgive nothing' (ничего + не yields negation). простит is future perfective 'will forgive' (he/she will not pardon anything).
segments.p1-3-g138: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'it seems' used to soften/assert probability; it stands alone as an introductory comment.
segments.p1-3-g139: и мстить начнет places the infinitive мстить before начнет for stylistic emphasis; начнет (future) + infinitive expresses 'will begin to take revenge'. The inversion (infinitive first) stresses the action.
segments.p1-3-g140: но как-нибудь урывками is a manner phrase: но как-нибудь = 'but somehow / in some way' (нибудь softens/indefinitizes как) and урывками (instrumental/adv.) means 'in snatches' or 'sporadically' describing how she will act.
segments.p1-3-g141: мелочами is instrumental plural meaning 'by petty/small things' or 'in small ways' — another adverbial specifying manner.
segments.p1-3-g142: из-за печки is a compound idea 'from behind the stove'; here expressed as из + за + печки (genitive) to show origin/location, implying covert, domestic actions taken from a hidden place.
segments.p1-3-g143: инкогнито is an adverb borrowed from French/Latin meaning 'incognito / anonymously' and modifies the preceding manner phrases.
segments.p1-3-g144: не веря is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) 'not believing'; ни своему праву мстить continues the negative series ('not believing either in her own right to revenge') — веря (верить) takes the dative, so своему праву is dative, and мстить is the infinitive 'to take revenge.'
segments.p1-3-g145: ни успеху своего мщения continues the 'ни ... ни ...' negation: 'nor in the success of her vengeance' (успеху in dative governed by веря; мщения is genitive). и зная наперед is another adverbial participle 'and knowing beforehand' (наперед = 'in advance').
segments.p1-3-g146: что introduces the content clause 'that ...'; от всех своих попыток отомстить is a circumstantial phrase 'from all her attempts to take revenge' (от + genitive), where отомстить is the perfective infinitive 'to take revenge'; сама выстрадает is future perfective 'she herself will suffer' (выстрадать conveys suffering/obtaining by suffering); во сто раз больше того is a comparative measure 'a hundred times more than that', with того (genitive) pointing forward to the person referenced in the next segment.
segments.p1-3-g147: кому мстит is a relative/clarifying dative clause 'than the one (whom) she is revenging against' — кому is dative 'whom', and мстит is present tense here because it's a general description 'she takes revenge on (whom)'.
segments.p1-3-g148: а тот contrasts with the previous clause: a (but/while) тот = 'and that one / the other person' (demonstrative), preparing the concluding comment about that person's reaction.
segments.p1-3-g149: пожалуй is again a mitigating/hedging particle 'perhaps' (softens the assertion about the other's reaction).
segments.p1-3-g150: и не почешется is a colloquial dismissal: и 'and / even' + не почешется (future or general present/future of почесаться) 'will not even scratch himself / won't bother' — почешется is reflexive and here idiomatically means 'won't care / won't lift a finger.'
segments.p1-3-g151: На смертном одре is a prepositional phrase meaning "on the deathbed"; одре is an archaic/poetic form of "bed" and смертном is its adjective in the same case. Опять-таки is an adverbial phrase (опять + таки) combining "again" with an emphatic/insistent particle ("after all/still"). Все is the direct object "everything," and припомнит is a perfective future (3rd person singular) of припомнить meaning "will recall/remember."
segments.p1-3-g152: С + инструментал (с накопившимися процентами) means "with" something accompanying or added; накопившимися is a past passive/participle in instrumental plural agreeing with процентами and expresses "having accumulated". За все время uses за + accusative to mean "over/during the whole time." The final и is a trailing, colloquial conjunction ("and/also") that gives the phrase an emphatic, incomplete feel ("and.../too").
segments.p1-3-g51: 'И' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' introducing the clause; 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'тем более' is a fixed comparative phrase meaning 'all the more'; 'убежден' is a short-form adjective used predicatively meaning 'convinced' (state/result); 'в злом' is a prepositional/locative complement 'in (an) evil' where 'злом' is a nominalized/attributive form of 'зло' (here functioning as part of the phrase 'в злом подозрении' = 'in an evil suspicion').
segments.p1-3-g52: 'так сказать' is an idiomatic parenthetical 'so to speak / as it were' (literally 'so to say'); it softens or signals the figurative quality of what follows.
segments.p1-3-g53: The noun 'подозрении' (prepositional case) is the head of the phrase 'в ... подозрении' and means 'suspicion' or 'a hunch'; grammatically it completes 'убежден в ...' ('convinced in/by ...').
segments.p1-3-g54: 'что если' combines the complementizer 'что' ('that') with the conditional particle 'если' ('if'), introducing a subordinate conditional/example clause ('that if ...').
segments.p1-3-g55: 'например' is an adverb meaning 'for example' marking the following case as illustrative.
segments.p1-3-g56: 'взять' here functions as a verbal imperative/infinitival idea 'to take/assume' in the sense 'suppose one takes'; 'антитез' is the direct object 'the antithesis'; 'нормального человека' is a genitive attribute 'of a normal man' ('the antithesis of a normal man').
segments.p1-3-g57: 'то есть' is the explanatory phrase 'that is (to say)'; it introduces an apposition specifying the antithesis: 'человека усиленно сознающего' — 'a man intensely conscious.' 'усиленно' is an adverb modifying the participle 'сознающего' (present active participle modifying 'человека' in accusative).
segments.p1-3-g58: 'вышедшего' is a past passive/qualifying participle ('who has come out / who proceeded'), agreeing with 'человека' (accusative animate form looks like genitive); it begins a relative participial phrase describing the preceding 'man.'
segments.p1-3-g59: 'конечно' is a parenthetical adverb 'of course' marking the forthcoming qualification as expected or obvious.
segments.p1-3-g60: The phrase 'не из лона природы' negates origin: 'not from the womb/bosom of nature.' 'из' governs the genitive 'лона' and 'природы' (poetic 'лона' = 'womb/bosom'); together they emphasize artificial rather than natural origin.
segments.p1-3-g61: 'а' marks contrast 'but/rather'; 'из реторты' 'from a retort' (literal laboratory vessel) is the contrasted origin; the parenthesis 'это уже почти мистицизм' states 'this is already almost mysticism' — 'уже почти' = 'already almost'.
segments.p1-3-g62: 'господа' is a vocative/form of address 'gentlemen' (used to address the audience), here confirming the parenthetical aside.
segments.p1-3-g63: 'но' introduces a mild counterpoint 'but'; 'я подозреваю и это' = 'I suspect this as well' — 'и' adds 'also/even', so the narrator admits he even suspects this somewhat extravagant idea.
segments.p1-3-g64: 'то' resumes the result 'then/that (being so)'; 'этот ретортный человек' = 'this retort-made/experimental man'; 'до того' here is an adverbial phrase meaning 'to such an extent / so far'; 'иногда' 'sometimes'; 'пасует перед' is a colloquial/older verb phrase meaning 'gives way before / falters before / is daunted by'; 'своим антитезом' is the instrumental 'before his antithesis.'
segments.p1-3-g65: 'что' introduces the result clause 'that'; 'сам себя' ('he himself') is emphatic reflexive: 'he himself (himself) ...' — 'сам' intensifies the subject acting upon 'себя' (himself).
segments.p1-3-g66: 'со всем своим усиленным сознанием' is an instrumental/ablative-type phrase 'with all his intensified consciousness' ('со' variant of 'с' used before certain consonant clusters); grammatically it modifies the verb 'считает' that follows, specifying the mental state in which he judges himself.
segments.p1-3-g67: 'добросовестно' is an adverb 'conscientiously / in good faith'; 'считает' is the finite verb 'considers/supposes'; 'считать за' is the verbal phrase 'to consider as' taking the object 'мышь' (mouse).
segments.p1-3-g68: 'а не за человека' provides the contrastive complement: 'and not as a man.' 'а' = 'but/rather', 'за' keeps the 'consider as' construction, 'человека' is the opposed noun in accusative (animate).
segments.p1-3-g69: 'Пусть' introduces a concessive/hypothetical clause 'let it be / suppose'; 'это' 'this'; 'и' here adds concessive emphasis 'even/though'; 'усиленно сознающая' is a participial phrase 'intensely conscious' modifying 'мышь' (present active participle); overall: 'Let that be an intensely self-conscious mouse.'
segments.p1-3-g70: 'но всё-таки' (split here as 'но все-таки') is a fixed adversative adverb meaning 'nevertheless / all the same'; 'мышь' restates the thing is still a mouse despite the previous qualification.
segments.p1-3-g71: 'а тут человек' contrasts the mouse with 'here (is) a man' — 'а' marks contrast, 'тут' 'here/at this point', 'человек' 'a man/person' (subject implied).
segments.p1-3-g72: 'а следственно' is an elliptical connective 'and consequently' or 'and therefore' indicating an implied conclusion follows; the final dot/dash signals the thought is left to inference.
segments.p1-3-g75: Comma (punctuation) separating clauses; no lexical content.
segments.p1-3-g76: 'и проч.' is an abbreviated colloquial phrase ('и прочее' / 'и прочее тому подобное') meaning 'and the rest / and so forth / etc.'; used to truncate a list or implication.
segments.p1-3-g77: 'И' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this sentence to the preceding remarks; short connective opening.
segments.p1-3-g78: 'главное' is a nominative nominal phrase meaning 'the main thing / above all' and functions as a discourse marker emphasizing what follows.
segments.p1-3-g79: 'он сам' places emphasis on the subject: 'he himself' (безилличное усиление); 'сам' is emphatic, not merely reflexive here.
segments.p1-3-g80: The repeated 'сам' intensifies 'he himself'; 'ведь' is a particle 'after all / you see' adding an explanatory tone; 'считает себя за мышь' is the verb phrase 'considers himself to be a mouse' with 'считать за' meaning 'to regard as' and 'себя' the reflexive object.
segments.p1-3-g81: 'его' here is the accusative/object pronoun 'him' (not the possessive 'his'); 'об этом' is the prepositional phrase 'about this' or 'concerning that'; 'никто не просит' is the negated clause 'no one asks/requests' — together: 'no one asks him about this' (i.e. he is not compelled by others to call himself a mouse).
segments.p1-3-g82: 'а это важный пункт' closes the thought: 'and this is an important point' — 'а' contrasts/introduces, 'это' points to the preceding fact, 'важный пункт' 'important point/item.'
segments.p1-3-g242: The token 'д.' is an abbreviation. Here it functions as the final abbreviated element (part of 'и т. д.' / 'и так далее') and is written with a period. The listed senses do not include 'далее', so the exact abbreviated meaning is not represented in the provided sense list.
segments.p1-3-g243: This segment is a simple comma punctuation mark separating items; it does not contain lexical words and only serves to join the preceding and following fragments.
segments.p1-3-g244: 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' joining items in a list; here it functions as the first word of the fixed abbreviation 'и т. д.' ('and so on'). 'т.' is an abbreviated form (in the full phrase it stands for 'так'), but the provided senses list does not include that meaning.
segments.p1-3-g245: As in earlier segments, 'д.' is an abbreviation (final element of 'и т. д.') and here the token also includes a closing quotation mark. The provided senses do not contain the intended expansion ('далее'), so no matching sense index is available.
segments.p1-3-g247: 'Господи боже' is an exclamatory vocative phrase: 'Господи' and 'боже' are old-fashioned/vocative forms used to invoke or exclaim to God (equivalent to 'Good Lord' / 'God!'). It functions as an interjection setting a strong emotional tone.
segments.p1-3-g248: The phrase 'да какое мне дело до ...' is a colloquial set expression meaning 'what business is it of mine concerning ...'. 'да' here is an expletive/discourse particle adding emphasis, 'какое' = 'what', 'мне' is the dative experiencer 'to me', and 'дело до' takes a genitive object ('до законов ...') meaning 'concerning/regarding'. The coordinated nouns 'законов природы и арифметики' are both genitives governed by 'до'.
segments.p1-3-g249: This subordinate clause ('когда ... не нравятся?') gives the condition/reason. 'когда' = 'when', 'мне' again marks the experiencer, 'почему-нибудь' ('почему' + 'нибудь') here means 'for some reason' or 'why-ever' with 'нибудь' functioning as a softening/indefinite particle, 'эти законы' is the nominative plural subject (but placed after the dative for emphasis), and 'и' before 'дважды' is emphatic 'even/also'. The verbless phrase 'дважды два четыре' expresses 'two times two (is) four'—Russian allows this terse equational structure with the predicate omitted or understood.
segments.p1-3-g194: Эти господа is a demonstrative + noun phrase in the nominative plural ('these gentlemen'); при иных казусах is a prepositional phrase using при + locative plural (казусах) meaning 'in/under certain other cases'. The order places the topical referent before the circumstance.
segments.p1-3-g195: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' that comments on the preceding phrase; it does not affect case or agreement.
segments.p1-3-g196: Хотя и ревут is a concessive clause: хотя (conjunction 'although' / here 'even though') + particle и (an emphatic/‘even’ use) + ревут (3rd person plural present indicative 'they roar'). The и after хотя intensifies the concession.
segments.p1-3-g197: Как быки is a simile: как ('like/as') followed by nominative plural быки ('bulls'), modifying ревут: 'they roar like bulls.'
segments.p1-3-g198: Во все горло is an idiomatic adverbial phrase meaning 'at the top of one's lungs' or literally 'with the whole throat'. Во is the form of в used before a vowel and governs the accusative phrase все горло.
segments.p1-3-g199: Хоть это begins another concessive turn: хоть here functions 'even if/although', with это ('this/it') pointing back to the roaring; it sets up the clause that follows.
segments.p1-3-g200: Положим is a parenthetical verb form meaning 'let us suppose' or 'let's say' (used here to introduce a hypothetical/illustrative remark).
segments.p1-3-g201: И приносит им величайшую честь: приносит is 3rd person singular present 'brings/produces', им is the dative 'to them', величайшую честь is the accusative object 'the greatest honour'; the particle и before the verb gives a concessive/emphatic sense ('even brings').
segments.p1-3-g202: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but' introducing the contrast with what follows.
segments.p1-3-g203: Как уже сказал я is a parenthetical reference to a prior remark: как ('as') + уже ('already') + сказал (past masculine singular of 'say') + я (I) — word order places я after the verb for emphasis: 'as I have already said.'
segments.p1-3-g204: Перед невозможностью uses перед + instrumental (невозможностью) to mean 'faced with / before an impossibility'; они is the subject 'they', тотчас is an adverb 'immediately', смиряются is 3rd person plural present reflexive 'to humble/ resign oneself' — here 'they immediately resign themselves.'
segments.p1-3-g205: Невозможность is the nominative subject 'impossibility'; the dash functions as a copular pause; значит is 3rd person singular present 'means/does (it) mean' forming the question, and каменная стена is the predicate nominative 'a stone wall' (adjective + noun).
segments.p1-3-g206: Какая is an interrogative adjective 'what/which' agreeing with the feminine noun стена (nominative singular); каменная стена is the noun phrase 'stone wall' used here as a terse, skeptical question.
segments.p1-3-g207: Ну is a discourse particle 'well' used to introduce or soften the speaker's next remark.
segments.p1-3-g208: Разумеется is an adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', marking the following list as obvious or expected.
segments.p1-3-g209: Законы природы is a genitive phrase 'the laws of nature': законы (nominative plural) 'laws' and природы (genitive singular) 'of nature' — a headed noun phrase listing one of the items.
segments.p1-3-g210: Выводы естественных наук is another list item: выводы (nominative plural 'conclusions/inferences') + естественных (genitive plural adjective 'natural') + наук (genitive plural 'sciences') = 'the conclusions of the natural sciences'.
segments.p1-3-g211: Математика is nominative singular and here names the subject-area 'mathematics', the final item in the list; despite the lemma/senses supplied, context shows it functions as the discipline 'mathematics'.
segments.p1-3-g229: The clause is an impersonal / passive-feeling statement: Природа (nominative subject) + вас (direct object) + не (negation) + спрашивается (reflexive verb). Here спрашивается is used in a passive/impersonal sense meaning "is asked" or "consulted," so the whole phrase means "Nature does not ask/consult you."
segments.p1-3-g230: This is the fixed impersonal idiom ей дела нет до X: "there is no matter/concern to her about X." Ей is the dative pronoun "to it/her," дела нет is a set phrase "there is no concern/matter," and до introduces the domains (до ваших желаний, до того) that follow.
segments.p1-3-g231: This continues the objects introduced by до: нравятся (present plural of нравиться) governs вам (dative) and takes ее законы as the thing that could "please" you. The enclitic particle ль (= ли) marks an indirect question "whether," so нравятся ль вам ... или не нравятся = "whether you like ... or do not like (them)."
segments.p1-3-g232: Вы обязаны + infinitive (принимать) is a modal construction meaning "you are obliged/you must accept." Ее is the direct object (referring back to "nature"), and так indicates the manner "in that way / thus," linking to the following clause.
segments.p1-3-g233: Как introduces a comparative/subordinate clause of manner: как она есть = "as she/it is." Есть is the present (used here for emphasis) of быть, so the clause means "exactly as it exists."
segments.p1-3-g234: А is a contrasting/coordinating conjunction here, and следственно is an adverb meaning "consequently/therefore;" together they signal a consequence derived from the previous clause.
segments.p1-3-g235: И joins the consequence: все ("all") modifies результаты ("results, consequences"). Ее is the possessive reference to the feminine noun (nature), so the phrase means "and all its results."
segments.p1-3-g236: Single nominative noun Стена as the topic/subject; the comma marks a rhetorical pause before the parenthetical connective.
segments.p1-3-g237: Значит here is a parenthetical adverb meaning "then/that means" (used rhetorically) and is set off by commas.
segments.p1-3-g238: И есть combines the particle и (used here as an intensifier: "indeed/precisely") with the present copula есть (to be) to state the identity emphatically: "is indeed a wall." Стена is repeated as the predicate nominative.
segments.p1-3-g241: И т. is an abbreviated shorthand for "и так далее" ("and so on / etc."). И is the coordinating conjunction "and," and the following abbreviated t. stands in for the rest of the phrase; the whole functions as an ellipsis marker.
segments.p1-3-g181: "Но" is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however'. "Успокойтесь" is the 2nd-person-plural (or polite singular) imperative of успокоиться (reflexive), a command: 'calm down'. The comma marks direct address.
segments.p1-3-g182: "господа" is a vocative/address form 'gentlemen' (plural of господин) used to address the listeners; placed after the imperative as a polite or ironic address.
segments.p1-3-g183: Subject "я" comes before the negated verb; "не получал" is past imperfective (получать) with negation, a neutral past statement meaning 'I did not receive' or 'I didn't get (slaps)'. "пощечин" is the genitive plural (partitive) used after negation: 'any slaps'.
segments.p1-3-g184: "хотя" introduces a concessive clause 'although'. The impersonal phrase "мне всё равно" (dative + "всё равно") means 'it is all the same to me / I don't care'; "совершенно" is an adverbial intensifier 'absolutely/completely'. Word order places the dative "мне" before the predicate.
segments.p1-3-g185: The clause "как бы вы об этом ни думали" is a concessive subordinate clause formed by the "как бы ... ни" pattern: "как" + particle "бы" + verb in past form expresses 'however' or 'no matter how'. "вы" is the subject; "об этом" is 'about this' (prepositional + demonstrative), "ни" is the emphasising particle used with the concessive construction, and "думали" is the past form used with "бы" to mark a hypothetical/contrary-to-fact shade: 'however you may think about it'.
segments.p1-3-g186: "Я" is the pronoun 'I', here fronted and set off by a comma as the grammatical subject before the parenthetical material.
segments.p1-3-g187: "может быть" is a parenthetical modal expression meaning 'perhaps/maybe'; it is composed of может (possibility 'may') + infinitive быть ('to be') and functions adverbially to hedge the assertion.
segments.p1-3-g188: "еще" here means 'even/still' and adds emphasis; "сам-то" is an emphatic/contrastive combination (сам = 'myself' / emphatic, -то is a particle that stresses contrast or insistence), and "жалею" is 1st-person present 'I regret'. Together they mean roughly 'I even myself (indeed) regret'.
segments.p1-3-g189: "что" introduces the subordinate content clause 'that...'. "в мою жизнь" is a temporal/experiential accusative phrase 'in my life' (literally 'into my life' used as 'during my life'). "мало" modifies the verb as an adverb/quantifier meaning 'too little/insufficiently'. "роздал" (раздать in the past) is 'gave out/distributed' in past masculine, and "пощечин" is the genitive plural (partitive) 'slaps' — together: 'gave out too few slaps'.
segments.p1-3-g190: "Но" = 'but/however' and begins the sentence; "довольно" here means 'enough' or 'that's enough' (an interjection/command to stop the topic), rather than 'quite'.
segments.p1-3-g191: "ни слова больше" is a fixed negative imperative phrase 'not a word more / no more words' ("ни" emphasises negation, "слова" is 'word' in the partitive/genitive after negation, "больше" 'more/no longer'). "об этой ... теме" is a prepositional phrase 'about this topic'; the adjective phrase "чрезвычайно для вас интересной" places the adverb "чрезвычайно" ('extremely') before the dative phrase "для вас" ('for you') modifying "интересной" ('interesting').
segments.p1-3-g192: "Продолжаю" is 1st-person-singular present 'I continue' with the subject implied or previously stated; "спокойно" is an adverb 'calmly' modifying the verb. "о людях" is the prepositional object 'about people', and "с крепкими нервами" is an instrumental phrase 'with strong/robust nerves' modifying which people are meant.
segments.p1-3-g193: "не понимающих" is a present active participle with negation ('not understanding' / 'who do not understand') and functions as a relative clause modifying "людях"; it agrees in number/case with that noun. "известной" here means 'a certain/particular' (not 'well-known') and together with the genitive "утонченности наслаждений" ('refinement/subtlety of pleasures') forms the object of "понимающих": 'who do not understand a certain subtlety/refinement of pleasures.'
segments.p1-3-g212: 'Уж' is a colloquial particle adding emphasis; 'как' here introduces a concessive/temporal clause meaning 'if/when' (colloquial 'as soon as/if'); 'докажут' is 3rd person plural future/present form of 'доказать' meaning 'they will prove/show'; 'тебе' is the dative 'to you' (the indirect object). Together this gives 'if/when they prove to you...'.
segments.p1-3-g213: 'например' is an adverbial parenthetical meaning 'for example' and simply colors the clause as an instance.
segments.p1-3-g214: 'что' introduces a subordinate (reported) clause 'that...'; 'от' is the preposition 'from'; 'обезьяны' is the genitive 'of an ape/monkey'; 'произошел' is the masculine singular past of 'произойти' used elliptically (the subject 'you' is omitted) meaning '(you) originated/descended (from)'.
segments.p1-3-g215: 'так уж и' is an emphatic colloquial string ('so/then indeed') leading into an impersonal construction; 'нечего' + infinitive expresses impersonal 'there is nothing to (do)/no point (in)'; 'морщиться' is the infinitive 'to frown/grimace' — so the phrase means 'there's no point in frowning.'
segments.p1-3-g216: 'Принимай' is the 2nd-person imperative of 'принимать' meaning 'accept' (addressing the reader/listener); 'как есть' is an idiomatic phrase 'as it is' (literally 'as [it] is', with 'есть' as the copula).
segments.p1-3-g217: Same construction as before: 'Уж' is an emphatic particle; 'как' introduces the conditional/temporal idea 'if/when'; 'докажут' is 3rd person plural (they will prove); 'тебе' is dative 'to you' — 'if/when they prove to you...'.
segments.p1-3-g218: 'что' is the subordinating conjunction 'that', beginning the reported content clause.
segments.p1-3-g219: 'в сущности' is a parenthetical adverbial phrase meaning 'in essence' or 'essentially': 'в' (preposition) + 'сущности' (noun in prepositional case 'essence' used adverbially).
segments.p1-3-g220: This long clause is the main content: 'одна капелька твоего собственного жиру' = 'a single drop of your own fat' (noun phrase with genitive modifiers); 'тебе должна быть' — impersonal/modal construction where 'должна' (feminine to agree with 'капелька') + infinitive 'быть' gives 'ought to be' or 'should be to you' with the dative 'тебе'; 'дороже ста тысяч тебе подобных' uses the comparative 'дороже' + genitive 'ста тысяч' ('than a hundred thousand') and 'тебе подобных' ('similar to you') as the standard of comparison. Then 'и что в этом результате разрешатся под конец все так называемые добродетели и обязанности и прочие бредни и предрассудки' — 'and that, in this result, in the end, all the so‑called virtues and duties and other nonsense and prejudices will be resolved': 'разрешатся' is plural reflexive/passive-future meaning 'will be resolved/settled', 'под конец' = 'at last/in the end', 'так называемые' = 'so-called' (present participle used adjectivally).
segments.p1-3-g221: 'так уж так и' is a colloquial, somewhat redundant string used for emphasis ('so indeed, then'); 'принимай' is the imperative 'accept' addressing the reader — the speaker's conclusion from the proved premises.
segments.p1-3-g222: 'нечего делать' is an impersonal phrase meaning 'there is nothing to be done' or 'no use/point in taking action'; the enclitic particle 'то' at the end is colloquial and adds emphasis/irritation (roughly 'anyway' or 'after all').
segments.p1-3-g223: 'потому' is an adverb meaning 'therefore/for that reason'; 'дважды два — математика' is a short declarative sentence: literally 'two times two — mathematics', i.e. an appeal to the incontrovertible certainty of arithmetic as the final, undeniable authority. (Note: the lemma given is 'математик' but here the noun used is 'математика' meaning 'mathematics'.)
segments.p1-3-g224: Попробуйте is an imperative (polite/plural form) of попробовать meaning "try." It is normally followed by an infinitive to express an invitation or challenge. Возразить is the perfective infinitive meaning "to object/to oppose;" together they form a standard "try to [do something]" construction.
segments.p1-3-g225: Помилуйте is an imperative plural/polite form of помиловать used as an exclamation meaning "have mercy!" or "forgive me!" — here it's an interjection addressing the speaker's claim.
segments.p1-3-g226: Закричат is 3rd person plural future (perfective) of закричать, meaning "they will cry out/shout." Вам is the dative "to you" indicating the addressee of the shouts. Word order and commas reproduce direct speech punctuation.
segments.p1-3-g227: Восставать is the imperfective infinitive "to rise up/rebel" used here as the verbal noun in an impersonal statement with нельзя. Нельзя is an impersonal predicate meaning "it is not allowed/one mustn't/cannot"; together they express the prohibition or impossibility of rebelling.
segments.p1-3-g228: Это here functions as a demonstrative copular element "this/that is," followed by a colloquial formula (дважды два четыре) where the verb 'equals' is omitted; the phrase asserts that the claim is as certain as "two times two equals four." Дважды is the adverb "twice" modifying the numerical expression.
segments.p1-3-g155: 'Но' introduces a contrast ('but'); 'именно вот' is an emphatic demonstrative sequence ('precisely/right here'); the phrase 'в этом холодном' is a prepositional phrase (в + demonstrative pronoun + adjective in prepositional singular) meaning 'in this cold...'.
segments.p1-3-g156: Two adjectives/nouns in the same prepositional case: 'омерзительном полуотчаянии' is prepositional singular neuter, adjective + noun, 'in the loathsome half-despair'.
segments.p1-3-g157: A single noun in the same case continuing the list: 'полувере' (prepositional) — 'half-belief', parallel to the previous noun phrase.
segments.p1-3-g158: 'в этом' repeats the demonstrative prepositional frame; 'сознательном погребении' is a noun phrase with an adjective (сознательный used in the sense 'deliberate'); 'самого себя' is an emphatic reflexive in the genitive (literally 'of oneself/the very self'); 'заживо' is an adverb 'alive'; 'с горя' uses 'с' meaning 'from' + genitive 'горя' = 'from grief'.
segments.p1-3-g159: 'в подполье' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in the underground/undercroft' (figurative here); 'на сорок лет' is a duration expression using 'на' + accusative meaning 'for forty years'.
segments.p1-3-g160: A long attributive phrase in the same prepositional frame: 'в этой ... безвыходности своего положения' — participial adjective 'усиленно созданной' ('intensively created') agrees with 'безвыходности' (prepositional), 'все-таки' (split into two tokens) is a particle meaning 'nevertheless/after all', 'отчасти сомнительной' = 'partly doubtful', and 'своего положения' is a genitive phrase 'of one's own situation'.
segments.p1-3-g161: 'во всем этом' (variant of 'в' used before consonant clusters) + demonstrative = 'in all this'; 'яде' is the prepositional of 'яд' used figuratively as 'poison'; it governs the genitive plural 'неудовлетворенных желаний' ('of unsatisfied desires').
segments.p1-3-g162: Past passive participle 'вошедших' (genitive plural) + adverb 'внутрь' form a participial modifier meaning 'that entered inward'; it agrees in case/number with the noun it modifies (желаний).
segments.p1-3-g163: 'во всей этой лихорадке' is prepositional ('in this whole fever'); 'лихорадке' is used metaphorically and takes the genitive plural 'колебаний' ('of vacillations/hesitations').
segments.p1-3-g164: A compound nominal phrase in genitive plural: 'принятых навеки решений' ('decisions adopted for ever') and 'через минуту опять наступающих раскаяний' ('repentances that recur a minute later') — both participial constructions modify a following abstract noun; the main verb is 'заключается' ('consists in') and its subject is 'сок' (figurative 'essence').
segments.p1-3-g165: 'о котором' is a prepositional relative clause ('about which'); 'я говорил' is the past tense of 'говорить' ('I spoke/said'), completing the relative clause that refers back to 'того странного наслаждения'.
segments.p1-3-g166: 'Оно' is a neuter pronoun 'it' as subject; 'до того' is an idiomatic intensifier meaning 'to such a degree/so'; 'тонкое' is an adjective in the predicate meaning 'subtle/delicate'.
segments.p1-3-g167: 'до того' repeats the degree intensifier; 'иногда' is 'sometimes'; 'не поддающееся' is a present passive participle meaning 'not susceptible' or 'not yielding' and takes dative/indirect object 'сознанью' (old form of 'сознанию') meaning 'to consciousness' — 'sometimes not susceptible to consciousness'.
segments.p1-3-g168: 'что' introduces a result clause ('that'); 'чуть-чуть ограниченные люди' = 'slightly limited people' (double 'чуть-чуть' = 'a little'); 'или даже просто люди с крепкими нервами' is an alternative group; 'не поймут' is future/plural negative 'will not understand'; 'в нем' = 'in it'; 'ни единой черты' = 'not a single feature' ('ни ... единой' is a negative quantifier).
segments.p1-3-g169: 'Может' here is an abbreviated modal adverb meaning 'maybe/perhaps' (short for 'может быть'); used colloquially to introduce a conjecture.
segments.p1-3-g170: 'еще и те' = 'even those/also those' (the particle 'еще' plus 'и' adds emphasis); 'не поймут' is future plural negative 'will not understand' — 'maybe even those will not understand'.
segments.p1-3-g171: 'прибавите вы от себя' is an ironic address to the reader: 'you will add on your own' — 'прибавите' is second person plural (imperative or future of address) and 'от себя' means 'from yourself/as your addition'.
segments.p1-3-g172: 'осклабляясь' is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'grinning/sneering', indicating a simultaneous manner in which the previous speech is uttered.
segments.p1-3-g173: Relative clause 'которые никогда не получали пощечин' = 'who have never received slaps'; 'получали' is past/imperfective 'received/underwent' used to mean 'have experienced'.
segments.p1-3-g174: 'и таким образом' = 'and in this way/thus'; 'вежливо намекнете мне' = 'you will politely hint to me' — 'намекнете' is future/imperative second person plural; the phrase is sarcastic/polite irony.
segments.p1-3-g175: 'что' begins a content clause: 'that I in my life...' — 'в мою жизнь' is an accusative time/existence expression meaning 'during my life' or 'in the course of my life'.
segments.p1-3-g176: 'может быть' is a parenthetical phrase meaning 'perhaps/maybe' (modal uncertainty); grammatically it's a compound formed from 'может' + infinitive 'быть'.
segments.p1-3-g177: 'тоже испытал пощечину' = 'also experienced a slap' — 'испытал' is past tense perfective 'experienced/underwent', 'пощечину' is accusative singular 'a slap'.
segments.p1-3-g178: 'а потому и говорю как знаток' = 'and therefore I speak as an expert' — 'потому и' gives causal emphasis ('that is why'), 'говорю' present tense first person, and 'как знаток' is a simile 'as an expert'.
segments.p1-3-g179: 'Бьюсь об заклад' is an idiomatic colloquial phrase meaning 'I bet' or 'I wager' (literally 'I strike against a stake'); grammatically it's a reflexive verb form used in a fixed expression followed by 'об заклад' ('on a wager').
segments.p1-3-g180: 'что' introduces the content clause; 'вы думаете' is present tense plural 'you think' and 'это' is the neuter demonstrative 'this/so' — 'that you think this/so'.
segments.p1-3-g250: Разумеется is an adverbial discourse marker meaning “of course” or “naturally,” used to introduce or emphasize the speaker’s attitude; it stands alone at the beginning of the sentence and does not affect case or verb forms that follow.
segments.p1-3-g251: Subject (я) + negated personal verb (не пробью). Пробью is the perfective future, 1st person singular (“I will break through/smash”), and with the negation не the direct object often appears in the genitive (такой стены) rather than the accusative — hence стены (gen.). Лбом is instrumental, indicating the means (“with my forehead”).
segments.p1-3-g252: A conditional clause introduced by если (“if”). и в самом деле is an emphatic phrase meaning “and/indeed in fact / really” (в самом деле = “really”); the construction сил не будет пробить is an impersonal way to say “there will be no strength to break through”: будет (future of быть) + infinitive пробить, with сил (genitive) indicating absence of strength.
segments.p1-3-g253: Contrastive coordinate clause introduced by но (“but”). и here is emphatic/augmentative (≈ “even” / “and besides”). Примирюсь is future tense, reflexive (примириться), first person singular: “I will reconcile / resign myself (to).” с ней uses preposition с + pronoun (her/it) to mean “with it.” потому только is a colloquial phrasing meaning “for that very reason / merely because.”
segments.p1-3-g254: That-clause introduced by что (“that / because”). У меня expresses possession (“I have …”); каменная стена is the possessed noun phrase (nominative). The second clause repeats possession (у меня) and uses the impersonal past form не хватило (хватить in past) with сил in genitive: literally “there was not enough strength” (= “I didn't have the strength”).
segments.p1-3-g255: Как будто introduces a comparison “as if / as though.” Такая каменная стена is the nominative subject. Есть is the present copula (often optional in Russian) meaning “is”; успокоение is the predicate noun “consolation/comfort.” Заключает в себе is a verb phrase “encloses / contains in itself,” where в себе is a reflexive locution “in itself.” Хоть какое-нибудь combines хоть (“at least”) + какое-нибудь (“some/any”) to form “at least some (word).” на мир uses на + noun to indicate the target/purpose here (“for peace” / “about peace”).
segments.p1-3-g256: A short parenthetical phrase: единственно только is a tautological doublet (both mean “only/merely”) used here for emphasis; потому (“for that reason”) links the clause that follows and explains the exclusive basis for the hypothetical peace.
segments.p1-3-g257: Subordinate clause introduced by что (“that”). Она refers back to the wall (“it”). The verbless fragment дважды два четыре is a colloquial statement of the arithmetic fact “two times two (is) four”; Russian frequently omits the verb (equals) in such formulaic phrases, so the meaning is “that it (is) two times two four.”
segments.p1-3-g258: О here is an exclamatory vocative particle (an archaic or poetic interjection meaning “O!”) used to call attention; нелепость (nominative) is the exclamative noun “absurdity” and нелепостей (genitive plural) intensifies it: literally “absurdity of absurdities” — a superlative exclamation formed by noun + genitive plural.
segments.p1-3-g259: То ли дело is an idiom meaning “that’s another matter” or “it’s a different thing” (то = that; ли is the enclitic interrogative/contrast particle). Все понимать uses the infinitive “to understand everything” and sets the rhetorical contrast to what was just described.
segments.p1-3-g260: A parallel infinitive phrase: все сознавать — “to be aware of / to acknowledge everything.” The repetition of все emphasizes totality.
segments.p1-3-g261: A noun phrase listing the objects of awareness: все невозможности (“all the impossibilities”) and каменные стены (“stone walls”). Все here functions distributively/collectively (“all”).
segments.p1-3-g262: Infinitive clause with negation: не примиряться — “not to reconcile / not to make peace.” Ни ... ни construction is implied; ни с одной из этих… uses ни + preposition + ordinal expression to mean “with none of these,” and из takes the genitive (из этих невозможностей).
segments.p1-3-g263: Conditional clause: если вам мерзит примиряться — “if it disgusts/repels you to reconcile.” Вам is dative (“to you”); мерзит is a present impersonal verb meaning “makes one feel repelled / disgusts”; infinitive follows.
segments.p1-3-g264: дойти путем ... до — a complex verb phrase “to reach / arrive (дойти) by means of (путем) the most inevitable logical combinations up to (до) the most repulsive conclusions.” The superlatives are formed with самых + adjective in genitive plural; на вечную тему uses на + accusative to mean “on the (eternal) theme of.”
segments.p1-3-g265: A that-clause: что даже и в каменной-то стене как будто чем-то сам виноват — “that even in the very stone wall one seems somehow to be guilty oneself.” Каменной-то uses the emphatic particle -то attached to the adjective (каменной) for emphasis; как будто = “as if;” чем-то is instrumental with -то meaning “by something / somehow”; сам виноват = emphatic predicate “is himself guilty.”
segments.p1-3-g266: Concessive phrase: хотя опять-таки до ясности очевидно — although (хотя) and again (опять-таки) it is obvious up to clarity (до ясности очевидно). До + abstract noun (ясности) expresses degree up to a point (“to the point of clarity”).
segments.p1-3-g267: What is obvious: что вовсе не виноват — a subordinate clause introduced by что, where вовсе не is a strong negation “not at all” and виноват is the predicative adjective “guilty.”
segments.p1-3-g268: И вследствие этого is a linking phrase: и = “and,” вследствие = “as a consequence / owing to,” этого = genitive pronoun “of this.” It signals consequence from the previous clause.
segments.p1-3-g269: A participial/gerund phrase describing manner: молча и бессильно скрежеща зубами — молча = “silently,” бессильно = “powerlessly,” скрежеща (present participle) + зубами (“with teeth”) = “gnashing/grinding the teeth.” The participle modifies the subject of the following main infinitive.
segments.p1-3-g270: Main infinitive phrase describing the resulting state: сладострастно замереть в инерции — сладострастно is an adverb (“voluptuously”), замереть is a perfective infinitive “to freeze / stand still,” and в инерции uses в + noun to indicate the state “in inertia” (figuratively: in passivity).
segments.p1-3-g271: A gerundial clause indicating motive or content of the dreaming: мечтая о том — мечтая is the gerund of мечтать (“dreaming / longing”), о том = “about that / that …” and introduces the content clause that follows.
segments.p1-3-g272: Content clause start: что даже и злиться — “that even being angry.” Here злиться is an infinitive/nominalized verb meaning “to be angry,” and даже и again adds emphasis (“even”).
segments.p1-3-g273: Выходит is a parenthetical verb meaning “it turns out / it comes out” (impersonal present): used to report the result or implication of what was just said.
segments.p1-3-g274: Tight idiom: тебе не на кого — literally “for you there is no one (to be angry at).” Тебе is dative (“for you”), не + на кого means “no one” as an object of anger (на кого — ‘at whom’).
segments.p1-3-g275: Another content clause: что предмета не находится — предмета is genitive (partitive/negation) used with не находится = “cannot be found / is not located.” The construction emphasizes absence of the object.
segments.p1-3-g276: A brief continuation: а может быть = “or perhaps / and maybe” (может быть is a fixed phrase meaning “perhaps”), used to suggest an alternate possibility.
segments.p1-3-g277: и никогда не найдется — “and perhaps it will never be found.” Найдется is the reflexive/future of найтись meaning “be found / turn up,” with никогда (never) and negation.
segments.p1-3-g278: A list of explanatory accusations introduced by что: тут подмен — “that here there is substitution” (подмен as noun: a replacement or fraudulent substitution). Тут = “here/at this point.”
segments.p1-3-g279: Single-word accusation: подтасовка — “tampering / rigging,” used as a noun to name a fraudulent manipulation.
segments.p1-3-g280: Another single-word accusation: шулерство — “cheating (in cards) / trickery,” again a noun naming fraudulent practice.
segments.p1-3-g281: что тут просто бурда — “that here it is simply drivel/rubbish.” Просто is an adverb “simply,” бурда is a pejorative noun (“drivel”).
segments.p1-3-g282: Dash plus a summative phrase: неизвестно что и неизвестно кто — literally “unknown what and unknown who,” an idiomatic way to say “who knows what and who” / “who knows what kind of thing and what kind of person.” Неизвестно here is impersonal.
segments.p1-3-g283: Но — simple coordinating conjunction “but,” marking the contrast to come.
segments.p1-3-g284: Несмотря на ... is a prepositional phrase meaning “despite / in spite of.” На takes the accusative (все эти неизвестности и подтасовки = “all these unknowns and tamperings”), and despite them introduces the main point that follows.
segments.p1-3-g285: У вас болит is the common Russian construction to express that someone has pain: у + person (вас, genitive) + болит (3rd person) = “you have (it) hurting / it aches.” Все-таки (here split as все and таки in the data) functions as an adverbial modifier meaning “after all / nevertheless” (emphasis).
segments.p1-3-g286: Comparative construction: и чем больше вам неизвестно — “and the more that is unknown to you” (чем introduces the comparative clause: the more …), where чем = “than / the more …” linking to the correlative тем in the next segment.
segments.p1-3-g287: Correlative comparative conclusion: тем больше болит — тем (“the more / so much the …”) + больше (“more”) + болит (“it aches / hurts”). Together: “the more (is unknown), the more it hurts.”

sentences.p1-3-s11: Well now, it is that very straightforward kind of man whom I consider a true, normal man — the sort the tender Mother Nature herself wanted to see, kindly bringing him into the world.
sentences.p1-3-s12: I envy such a man to the utmost bitterness.
sentences.p1-3-s13: He's stupid, I don't argue that with you, but maybe a normal person ought to be stupid, you know?
sentences.p1-3-s21: Now then, let us look at this mouse in action.
sentences.p1-3-s22: Suppose, for example, she is offended too (and she almost always is offended) and likewise wishes to take revenge.
sentences.p1-3-s23: There may, in fact, be even more malice in her than in l'homme de la nature et de la verité.
sentences.p1-3-s24: The nasty, base little wish to repay the offender with the same evil may perhaps gnaw at her even more horribly than it does in l'homme de la nature et de la verité, because l'homme de la nature et de la verité, from his congenital stupidity, counts his vengeance quite simply as justice; whereas the mouse, on account of intensified consciousness, here denies justice.
sentences.p1-3-s5: For them the wall is not an excuse, as, for example, it is for us — thinking people, and therefore doing nothing; not a pretext for turning back from the road, a pretext which our brother ordinarily does not himself believe in, but which he is always very glad of.
sentences.p1-3-s6: No — they give way (back down) with complete sincerity.
sentences.p1-3-s7: The wall has, for them, something calming, morally permissive and final — perhaps even something mystical.
sentences.p1-3-s25: At last it comes to the very matter — to the very act of revenge.
sentences.p1-3-s26: The poor mouse, besides that one original nastiness, had already managed to heap around herself, in the form of questions and doubts, so many other nastinesses; she had led one question on to so many unsolved questions that inevitably some fatal mess gathers around her, some stinking filth composed of her doubts, anxieties and, finally, of the spits hurled at her by the very actors who solemnly stand around like judges and dictators and laugh at her at the top of their lungs.
sentences.p1-3-s27: Of course, all that remains for her is to wave her paw at everything and, with a smile of affected contempt — which even she herself does not believe — shamefully slip into her little crevice.
sentences.p1-3-s28: There, in its nasty, stinking underground, our offended, beaten-down and mocked mouse immediately plunges into cold, poisonous and, above all, everlasting anger.
sentences.p1-3-s1: After all, with people who are able to avenge themselves and, generally, to stand up for themselves — how is that, for example, done?
sentences.p1-3-s2: After all, when, let us say, the feeling of revenge seizes them, then nothing else will remain in their whole being at that time except that feeling.
sentences.p1-3-s3: Such a gentleman simply charges straight for his goal, like an enraged bull lowering its horns, and only, perhaps, a wall stops him.
sentences.p1-3-s4: (By the way: before a wall such gentlemen, that is, straightforward people and men of action, sincerely falter.)
sentences.p1-3-s29: For forty years running she will keep recalling her grievance down to the very last, most shameful details, and at the same time each time will add still more shameful particulars of her own, maliciously teasing and irritating herself with her own imagination.
sentences.p1-3-s30: She herself will be ashamed of her imagination, but all the same she will recall everything, go over everything, invent tall tales about herself under the pretext that they too might have happened, and will forgive nothing.
sentences.p1-3-s31: Perhaps she'll even begin to take revenge, but somehow in snatches — by petty acts, from behind the stove, incognito — not believing either in her right to revenge or in the success of her vengeance and knowing in advance that from all her attempts to get even she herself will suffer a hundred times more than the one she is revenging against, and that one, perhaps, won't even bother.
sentences.p1-3-s32: On his deathbed he will, after all, remember everything again, with the interest accumulated over the whole time, too.
sentences.p1-3-s15: And I am all the more convinced, in an evil, so to speak, suspicion, that if, for example, one takes the antithesis of the normal man — that is, a man who is intensely conscious, who, of course, did not come from the womb of nature but from a retort (this is already almost mysticism, gentlemen, but I suspect that too) — then this retort-made man at times bows so far before his antithesis that he himself, with all his heightened consciousness, conscientiously considers himself a mouse and not a man.
sentences.p1-3-s16: Even if that is an intensely self-conscious mouse, it is still a mouse — whereas here is a man, and consequently...
sentences.p1-3-s20: And, above all, he himself — he himself, after all, considers himself a mouse; no one asks him about that; and that is an important point.
sentences.p1-3-s61: Good Lord, what business have I with the laws of nature and arithmetic when, for some reason, I don't like those laws — even that two times two is four?
sentences.p1-3-s43: These gentlemen, in certain other cases, for example, even though they roar like bulls at the top of their lungs — which, let us say, brings them the greatest honour — yet, as I have already said, when faced with an impossibility they immediately resign themselves.
sentences.p1-3-s44: Impossibility — does that mean a stone wall?
sentences.p1-3-s46: Well, of course — the laws of nature, the conclusions of the natural sciences, mathematics.
sentences.p1-3-s51: Nature does not consult you; it has no concern for your desires or for whether you like its laws or not.
sentences.p1-3-s52: You are obliged to accept it as it is, and, consequently, all its results.
sentences.p1-3-s53: A wall, then, is indeed a wall.
sentences.p1-3-s39: But calm down, gentlemen — I didn't receive any slaps, although it is absolutely all the same to me however you may think about it.
sentences.p1-3-s40: I, perhaps, even regret that in my life I have given out too few slaps.
sentences.p1-3-s41: But enough — not another word about this topic, which is extremely interesting to you.
sentences.p1-3-s42: I continue calmly about people with strong nerves who do not understand a certain refinement of pleasures.
sentences.p1-3-s47: If, for example, they prove to you that you descended from an ape, there's no point in frowning — accept it as it is.
sentences.p1-3-s48: If, for example, they prove to you that, in essence, a single drop of your own fat ought to be dearer to you than a hundred thousand like you, and that in this result, at the end, all the so‑called virtues and duties and other drivel and prejudices will be resolved, then accept it — there's nothing to be done; after all, two times two is mathematics.
sentences.p1-3-s50: "Have mercy!" they'll cry out to you, "you mustn't rise up: it's as plain as two times two is four!"
sentences.p1-3-s35: But it is precisely in this cold, loathsome half-despair, half-belief, in this deliberate burial of oneself alive from grief, in the underground for forty years, in this deliberately manufactured and yet partly doubtful hopelessness of one's situation, in all this poison of unsatisfied desires that have entered inward, in all this fever of vacillations, in decisions adopted for ever and repentances that come again a minute later — there lies the essence of that strange pleasure I spoke of.
sentences.p1-3-s36: It is so subtle, at times so imperceptible to consciousness, that slightly limited people, or even simply people with strong nerves, will not understand a single feature of it.
sentences.p1-3-s37: "Maybe even those won't understand," you'll add yourself, grinning, "who have never been slapped," — and in this way you will politely hint to me that I, in my life, perhaps also experienced a slap, and therefore speak as an expert.
sentences.p1-3-s38: I bet that you think so.
sentences.p1-3-s62: Of course, I won't smash my forehead through such a wall if there really won't be any strength to break through, but I still won't make peace with it merely because I have a stone wall and I didn't have the strength.
sentences.p1-3-s63: As if such a stone wall were truly a consolation and truly contained within itself at least some word for peace, solely for the reason that it is two times two four.
sentences.p1-3-s65: That’s a different matter: to understand everything, to be aware of everything — all the impossibilities and the stone walls; not to reconcile yourself to any one of these impossibilities and stone walls, if reconciling makes you sick; to arrive, by the route of the most inevitable logical combinations, at the most repulsive conclusions on the eternal theme that even in a stone wall one seems somehow to be guilty oneself, although, again, it is obvious up to clarity that one is not guilty at all, and because of that, silently and powerlessly gnashing your teeth, to voluptuously freeze in inertia, dreaming that even getting angry, it turns out, there is no one to be angry at; that the object cannot be found, and perhaps never will be found; that here there is substitution, tampering, cheating, that here it is simply drivel — unknown what and unknown who — but despite all these unknowns and tamperings, you nevertheless feel the pain, and the more is unknown to you, the more it hurts!
